categories.name: LET'S GO TO THE SCIENCE MUSEUM

questions.clue:
[IMAGE CLUE]
This two-word innovation was proposed by Lyman Spitzer in 1946 as an extraterrestrial observatory; we'll see infrared images from the Spitzer one
Kids love learning at this type of room where eggs produce life; change the subject when they ask where the chicks go next

[IMAGE CLUE]
There are some fun demos involving LIN, this stuff, & its 320-below-zero boiling point

[IMAGE CLUE]
This nearly 100-foot-long dinosaur is a favorite to display; Dippy was a beloved feature of London's Natural History Museum
By talking louder, I can increase this, the intensity of a wave, one of its 3 main qualities along with frequency & wavelength
Inisheer, Inishmaan & Inishmore are islands of this island nation
Spanish explorers thought these islands were the source of the gold a biblical king used to build a Jerusalem temple

[IMAGE CLUE]
The southernmost of Japan's four major islands, it's home to the historic port city of Nagasaki
"Ti" is pronounced like "S" in the language of this nation that extends from the Line Islands to the Gilberts

[IMAGE CLUE]
Following years of dispute, in 2025, the United Kingdom agreed to turn over the Chagos Archipelago to this island country

[IMAGE CLUE]
This composer to whom music came easy said the sonata heard here is "for beginners"
A beautiful piano piece by Debussy, it's named after a Verlaine poem & means "moonlight"
This Canadian set a new standard for Bach piano playing with 2 versions of the "Goldberg Variations", in the 1950s & '80s
He later wrote one down, but at the premiere of his third piano concerto Beethoven improvised this Italian-named solo section
A 1965 record by pianist Vladimir Horowitz popularized the many, many sonatas of this contemporary of Vivaldi
"A Fistful of Quarters" is the subtitle of a 2007 doc in which Billy Mitchell & Steve Wiebe vie to be "King" of this game

[IMAGE CLUE]
"Cancelled" is a 2025 documentary about this woman from Savannah who certainly was unafraid of adding butter to a recipe
In this 2013 film Tilikum was the name of the title killer whale that unfortunately lived up to its name by killing 3 people
Ken Burns teamed up with daughter Sarah for this 2012 documentary about a quintet of youths wrongfully convicted in NYC
Albert & David Maysles are known for this documentary on Edith Bouvier Beale & daughter Little Edie

[IMAGE CLUE]
In 2025, Andrzej Bargiel was the first to climb & ski down this mountain without supplemental oxygen; the Khumbu Icefall was tricky
2 firsts for the 1904 Olympics in this city: the first time the U.S. hosted & the first time gold medals were awarded
In 1868 this company named for 2 men made the first Swiss wristwatch, for a countess; men preferred to keep White-Rabbiting
The first person to see bacteria & protozoa with a microscope was this Dutchman in the 1670s
He was living in Bruges in the 1470s when he produced the first book printed in English
It's no compliment being likened to this insect of the genus Pediculus, a parasitic infester of the hair & skin
This word for a despicable person can rhyme with "staggered" instead of sounding out both words that go into it
Note to veggie lovers: this insult meaning "rascal" or "ne'er-do-well" contains green onion
Though no one knows the origin of this 3-syllable word for "fool", one theory involves a familiar Latin phrase meaning "not of sound mind"
Once a word for a freed slave, then a freethinker, it now refers to a rake, one who could do with a bit more restraint

final.clue: This 1653 work includes information on the care & use of proper equipment including lines, floats & rods
